problem: The current hlx self-update mechanism shells out to 'npm install -g git+https://github.com/Project-X-Innovation/helix-cli.git#main' which triggers 'tsc' via the prepare script, can fail when the build toolchain is unavailable (especially on Windows), and can brick the existing CLI installation on failure. Additionally, an auto-tag workflow pushes git tags on every main merge using a RELEASE_TOKEN, adding unwanted release friction. The ticket requires replacing the update path with prebuilt GitHub release assets, removing auto-tag, and ensuring failed updates never leave the CLI unusable.

files.why: Primary change target. Line 17 executes 'npm install -g git+https://...' via spawnSync — this is the exact mechanism being replaced with prebuilt artifact download.

Defines CANONICAL_REPO_URL, CANONICAL_BRANCH, GIT_INSTALL_SPEC constants (lines 3-9) and fetchRemoteSha() using 'git ls-remote' (lines 16-30). The constants are imported by perform.ts and index.ts. GIT_INSTALL_SPEC must be removed or repurposed.

Contains runUpdate() (lines 38-117) and checkAutoUpdate() (lines 124-185) — both orchestrate the update flow. Recovery messages on lines 89, 101 direct users to 'npm install -g git+https://...' and must change. Both functions call performUpdate() and validateInstall() which are being redesigned.

Post-update validation (lines 14-79) is npm-specific: resolves global npm root, checks for dist/index.js at the npm package path, runs 'node <binTarget> --version'. Must be redesigned for non-npm artifact layout.

Reads package.json version and appends commit SHA from config (lines 15-38). Version display format '1.3.4 (c8620a5)' is a ticket invariant. May need adjustment depending on how the prebuilt artifact stores version info.
CLI entry point and dispatcher. Line 69 defines SKIP_AUTO_UPDATE command set. Line 74 calls checkAutoUpdate() before dispatch. Lines 122-129 handle --version output with commit SHA display.

Defines InstallSource type (lines 5-11) with mode/repo/branch/commit/version fields. saveConfig() (lines 201-212) does read-merge-write to ~/.hlx/config.json. loadFullConfig() (lines 89-96) reads install metadata. The installSource schema may need extension for the new artifact channel.
TO BE REMOVED. Triggers on push to main (line 4), reads version from package.json, creates and pushes v{version} tags using secrets.RELEASE_TOKEN. Entire file is deleted per ticket requirements.

TO BE PRESERVED. Triggers on v* tag push (line 6), uses OIDC trusted publishing. Must remain available for intentional manual npm releases. No changes needed unless the build/validate steps can be shared with a new artifact workflow.
CLI documentation content. Lines 18 and 301 contain 'npm install -g git+https://...' instructions that must be replaced with the new GitHub artifact install path.
Error recovery message on line 15 references 'npm install -g git+https://...' — must be updated to new install instructions.
Error recovery message on line 25 references 'npm install -g git+https://...' — must be updated to new install instructions.

Defines bin entry (hlx -> dist/index.js), prepare script (npm run build -> tsc), files array, publishConfig. The prepare script is what causes the tsc requirement during 'npm install -g git+https://...' source installs. Build scripts (build, typecheck, test) define the CI validation steps.
TypeScript config targeting ES2022, module Node16, output to dist/. Defines the build that must happen in CI (not on user machines).
Excludes test files from npm package. Relevant to understanding what the prebuilt artifact should contain.
Command reference documentation. Lines 92-99 describe hlx update. May need updating to reflect the new update mechanism.
Existing test file. No update module tests exist — new tests will be needed for the replacement update mechanism.

facts.claim: Current update mechanism executes 'npm install -g git+https://github.com/Project-X-Innovation/helix-cli.git#main' via spawnSync in perform.ts line 17
The prepare script in package.json runs 'npm run build' which runs 'tsc', confirming that source-based installs require the TypeScript compiler on the user's machine
Remote SHA detection uses 'git ls-remote https://github.com/Project-X-Innovation/helix-cli.git refs/heads/main' — not the GitHub API
Install metadata is stored in ~/.hlx/config.json under the installSource field with type {mode: 'github'|'npm'|'unknown', repo?, branch?, commit?, version?}
auto-tag.yml triggers on push to main, requires secrets.RELEASE_TOKEN, and creates/pushes v{version} git tags
publish.yml triggers on v* tags and uses OIDC trusted publishing with npm provenance — no NPM_TOKEN secret needed
No existing GitHub release or artifact creation workflow exists — a new workflow for CI-built release artifacts must be created
facts.source: Only two workflow files exist: auto-tag.yml and publish.yml

facts.claim: validateInstall() resolves the global npm root path, checks for dist/index.js at '@projectxinnovation/helix-cli/dist/index.js', and runs 'node <path> --version' — this is entirely npm-path-dependent
Recovery messages in update/index.ts lines 89 and 101 direct users to 'npm install -g git+https://...' — these must change
The --version output already supports commit SHA display: format is '1.3.4 (c8620a5)' when installSource.commit exists in config
Pre-command auto-update via checkAutoUpdate() is called in src/index.ts:74, skipped for commands in SKIP_AUTO_UPDATE set: --version, -v, update, --help, -h, skill
HLX_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK env var is used for loop prevention during update subprocess calls
No tests exist for the update module — no src/update/*.test.ts files found
facts.source: Glob search for src/update/*.test.ts returned no results

facts.claim: Six source files contain hardcoded 'npm install -g git+https://...' references: perform.ts, check.ts, index.ts (update), cli-content.ts, show.ts, paths.ts
Package version is 1.3.4, package name is @projectxinnovation/helix-cli, bin entry is hlx -> dist/index.js
The package files array includes dist/ (excluding tests) and skill-content/ — these define what a prebuilt artifact should contain
The project is an ES module (type: module) targeting ES2022 with Node16 module resolution
Auto-update fails open (warns and continues) while manual hlx update fails closed (process.exit(1))
The saveConfig function does read-merge-write, preserving unrelated config fields when updating installSource or autoUpdate
No runtime inspection manifest found — no production runtime evidence available for this CLI tool repo

unknowns.question: What GitHub mechanism should serve the prebuilt artifact — GitHub Releases (with assets), or GitHub Actions workflow artifacts (which expire)?
unknowns.impact: GitHub Releases with assets are permanent and publicly addressable; workflow artifacts expire (default 90 days) and require authenticated API access. This fundamentally affects the download mechanism and auth requirements.

unknowns.question: What should the prebuilt artifact contain and how should it be structured — a tarball of the full package (dist/ + skill-content/ + package.json) or a self-contained binary?
unknowns.impact: Affects the artifact creation workflow, the unpack/validation logic in the updater, and how the CLI binary entrypoint works without npm's global bin linking.

unknowns.question: How will the installed CLI binary be linked/invoked without npm's global bin symlink mechanism?
unknowns.impact: Currently npm creates a symlink from a PATH location to dist/index.js. Without npm, the updater must handle PATH integration or replace files at a known install location.

unknowns.question: Is the GitHub repository private or public? This determines whether artifact downloads require authentication tokens.
unknowns.impact: Private repo requires GitHub token auth for release asset downloads. The error messaging for auth failures (a ticket requirement) depends on this.

unknowns.question: Should git ls-remote for SHA comparison be replaced with a GitHub API call, given that the release artifact already implies a GitHub API dependency?
unknowns.impact: git ls-remote requires git on the user's machine. If the update mechanism already uses GitHub API for artifact download, consolidating to API-only removes the git dependency.

unknowns.question: How should the staged-then-swap install mechanism work to guarantee atomicity — especially on Windows where file locking prevents replacing running executables?
unknowns.impact: The ticket requires that the live CLI is never replaced until validation passes. Windows file locking adds complexity to the swap step.

unknowns.question: What validation criteria are appropriate for a non-npm artifact? The current validateInstall() is entirely npm-path-dependent.
unknowns.impact: The validation approach must be redesigned. The ticket requires at minimum checking entrypoint existence and --version output.

unknowns.question: How will the CI workflow determine the commit SHA to embed in the release asset metadata?
unknowns.impact: The updater needs to compare remote vs local commit SHA. The release must associate a commit SHA for comparison.
